media.name: Mucin-containing minimal medium + 10 mM L-lactate (PMC10786315)
current_uncovered: inorganic mineral salts (unspecified salts/buffer, iron and trace elements)
media.base_medium: MCLMAN minimal medium (Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168 defined minimal medium) supplemented with 0.5% (w/v) partially purified gastric mucin
media.base_medium_reference: Alazzam B, Bonnassie-Rouxin S, Dufour V, Ermel G. MCLMAN, a new minimal medium for Campylobacter jejuni NCTC 11168. Res Microbiol 162:173-179 (2011). DOI 10.1016/j.resmic.2010.09.024; PMID 21144899. As applied (0.5% gastric mucin + carbon source) by the DiRita lab: Sinha R, LeVeque RM, Bowlin MQ, Gray MJ, DiRita VJ. Phosphate Transporter PstSCAB of Campylobacter jejuni Is a Critical Determinant of Lactate-Dependent Growth and Colonization in Chickens. J Bacteriol 202:e00716-19 (2020). DOI 10.1128/jb.00716-19; PMID 31932316; PMC7167465 (this is reference (1) cited in the PMC10786315 SI Appendix).
media.verification_evidence: We tested C. jejuni growth in mucin-containing minimal media supplemented with either 10 mM butyrate or 10 mM L-lactate and observed better growth with L-lactate in the media than with butyrate (Fig. 4A).
media.citation: Medium composition from PMC10786315 (see GrowthDB record).
